content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Linked Prompting: Explicit Citation Anchors to Reduce LLM Hallucination",
  "problem": "Large language models often generate plausible but ungrounded or hallucinated factual content, limiting their reliability for information-seeking tasks.",
  "motivation": "Mitigating hallucination in LLMs is critical for safe and trustworthy deployment, especially as their outputs are increasingly used in professional and scientific contexts. Recent research suggests LLMs struggle to distinguish between factual and invented details without external grounding, and manual prompt engineering only partially addresses the issue.",
  "method": "We propose Fact-Linked Prompting, in which the prompt explicitly instructs the LLM to generate answers with in-sentence citation anchors tied to provided reference snippets. The prompt provides several candidate reference snippets and requests the model to link each factual claim to a corresponding snippet (e.g., via [1], [2]). During generation, the LLM is penalized (via prompt constraint) for outputting facts without source linkage. This approach aims to reinforce factual grounding by tightly coupling model statements to evidence.",
  "experiment_plan": "On a subset of the TruthfulQA and HotpotQA datasets, compare baseline prompting to Fact-Linked Prompting. Metrics include factuality (human evaluation), hallucination rate, and citation precision. Baseline is standard instruction-following; for ablation, test both with and without explicit citation penalties. Statistical significance measured over 100 sampled questions per dataset."
}
